```

# /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the xorg.conf(5) manual page.

Section "Files"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/fonts/X11/misc"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/fonts/X11/cyrillic"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/fonts/X11/100dpi/:unscaled"

	Option		"XkbModel"	"pc104"
	Option		"XkbLayout"	"us"

EndSection

	Driver		"wacom"
	Identifier	"stylus"
	Option		"Device"	"/dev/input/wacom"

	Identifier	"cursor"

	Option		"Type"		"cursor"
	Option		"ForceDevice"	"ISDV4"		# Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"ATI Technologies Inc RV280 [Radeon 9200 SE]"
        Driver                "ati"
        BusID                "PCI:1:0:0"
        Option          "XAANoOffscreenPixmaps"
        Option "AGPMode" "4"

        Option "DisableGLXRootClipping" "true"

EndSection

Section "Monitor"

	HorizSync	30-70
	VertRefresh	50-160
EndSection

### Post by erm67 on 2007-04-27
Your log says dri is working:

drmOpenDevice: node name is /dev/dri/card0
#
drmOpenDevice: open result is 7, (OK)
....
#
(II) RADEON(0): [DRI] installation complete

Your user IS in the video group right?

try these commands and post the result:
id
ls -l /dev/dri

### Post by AquaFox90 on 2007-04-27
```
qusai@qusai-pc:~$ ls -l /dev/dri
total 0
crw-rw-rw- 1 root video 226, 0 2007-04-27 08:39 card0

```

---

### Post by erm67 on 2007-04-28
the other command was simply

```
id
```

that shows if you are in the video group, but you aren't so edit /etc/group 

```
gksudo gedit /etc/group
```

search the line that begins with video and put your user name there

```
utmp:x:43:
video:x:44:<your user name here>
sasl:x:45:
```

save and restart X

---

### Post by AquaFox90 on 2007-04-28
My user name was already in the video group, it's nothing like that. Check the log, I think it's loading the wrong DRI files?

### Post by blitzer on 2007-04-28
Hello AquaFox90,

Aiglx is the only driver we have for Feisty :(     Now if you want gaming get yourself an Nivida card !  [COLOR="Red"]The next options [/COLOR]would be wait for a fix on the  last supported ATI 9200SE - Fglrx 8.28 for Feisty (wait and see).  Download Edgy Eft so you can use the Fglrx 8.28 driver which worked flawlessly :)   [COLOR="Blue"]Aiglx is ok but not for gaming such as First Person Shooters.  The Proprietary Fglrx driver 8.28 worked the best. 
[/COLOR]
For Aiglx to work you have to use the terminal >Applications >Accessories >Terminal.  Type in Terminal:  ```
Sudo  dpkg-reconfigure xserver-xorg
```

Go through each screen using the defaults until you get to the section for your video card.  Choose [COLOR="Red"]ATI[/COLOR] - > You will have to use the space bar or arrow keys to select options.  Use all defaults except video card !

---

### Post by erm67 on 2007-04-29
try this:
[http://albertomilone.com/nvidia_scripts1.html](http://albertomilone.com/nvidia_scripts1.html)

I tried this envy and it rendered my system like yours i.e. no error messages but direct rendering no
this solved the problem:

sudo apt-get --reinstall install  libgl1-mesa-glx libgl1-mesa-dri libglu1-mesa libglu1-mesa

this is my sudo lspci -v

01:00.0 VGA compatible controller: ATI Technologies Inc RV280 [Radeon 9200] (rev 01) (prog-if 00 [VGA])
        Subsystem: PC Partner Limited Sapphire Radeon 9200
        Flags: bus master, VGA palette snoop, 66MHz, medium devsel, latency 64, IRQ 11
        Memory at d0000000 (32-bit, prefetchable) [size=256M]
        I/O ports at d800 [size=256]
        Memory at cf000000 (32-bit, non-prefetchable) [size=64K]
        Expansion ROM at cffe0000 [disabled] [size=128K]
        Capabilities: [58] AGP version 2.0
        Capabilities: [50] Power Management version 2

erm67@ubuntu:~$ glxinfo|grep rendering
direct rendering: Yes
